title: Find a doctor or nurse practitioner
text:
The official Ontario page explains how to find a family doctor, nurse practitioner, or specialist, and includes information about the Health Care Connect program.

The page says a family doctor or nurse practitioner is a primary health care clinician for new, non-emergency health concerns.

The page lists two options for finding a family doctor or nurse practitioner who is accepting new patients: register with Health Care Connect, or contact an Ontario physician to ask if they are accepting new patients.

The page says Health Care Connect can search for a family doctor or nurse practitioner accepting new patients in a person’s community. It also says joining Health Care Connect does not guarantee that a clinician will be found immediately.

Do not use this summary to decide eligibility or availability. The exact program requirements, contact methods, and current timelines must be read from the official page.

title: Apply for OHIP and get a health card
text:
The official Ontario page explains that the Ontario Health Insurance Plan (OHIP) pays for many health services people may need. It says people need to apply and, once approved, receive an Ontario health card that shows they are covered by OHIP.

The page includes information about health care in Ontario, what OHIP may cover, who may qualify, how to apply, reapplying, OHIP for babies, adopted children, military family members, visitors to Ontario, and requesting a review.

The page says OHIP applications are made in person at a ServiceOntario centre and that applicants need to bring a completed registration form and separate documents for status, Ontario residency, and identity.

Do not use this summary to decide eligibility. The exact eligibility rules, document requirements, exceptions, and appeal deadlines must be read from the official page.

title: Health
text:
Settlement.Org describes its Health section as information for newcomers about health insurance, finding a doctor or other medical professional, and health services for people and families.

The Health section links to topics including OHIP and health insurance, finding a doctor or medical professional, community and public health, mental health and addiction, emergency services, nutrition and healthy living, sexual and reproductive health, family health, refugee health, patients' rights and responsibilities, and disability.

The emergency services section includes newcomer-oriented information about hospital emergency department visits, getting help in an emergency, and finding a hospital.

Do not use this summary as official government guidance. Use Settlement.Org as a newcomer-friendly support source and verify healthcare rules against official Ontario sources.
